items.title: Erdogan's Police Storm CHP HQ with Tear Gas — Turkey Just Crossed from Legal to Physical
items.idea: Turkish riot police breached the main opposition party (CHP) headquarters in Ankara on May 24 using tear gas, evicting Özgür Özel's faction after a 3-day standoff. This operationalizes a court decision annulling the 2023 CHP congress — moving from legal invalidation (paper) to physical asset seizure (premises, payroll infrastructure, membership rolls). Turkish stocks dropped 6% intraday after the initial court move. The police action signals the game is about controlling the opposition's operating system — not a one-off verdict.
items.angle: The market angle is institutional intervention risk. If courts + police can reassign opposition party leadership overnight, investors must price a higher probability of abrupt administrative intervention across banks, universities, and businesses. The 6% stock drop was the initial reaction to the court move — the police raid is the confirmation.
items.tweetA: Turkish riot police stormed the main opposition party HQ with tear gas. Evicted the elected leadership. This is not a one-off — it's act three of a playbook: courts invalidate → police enforce → opposition loses its operating system. $TUR already down 6% on the court move alone.
items.tweetB: The market question on Turkey is not ideology. It's this: if courts and police can reassign opposition leadership overnight, what's the probability they do it to a bank, a university, or your business? That's why Turkish stocks fell 6% and the repricing is not done.

items.title: Turkey's 'Trusteeship Logic' — The Scalable State Asset Seizure Template
items.idea: Turkey has a mature administrative toolkit for placing institutions under state-appointed trustees when a court rules governance is 'legally defective.' The same week, Erdogan signed a decree closing Istanbul's Bilgi University — same playbook, different target. Pattern: court frames governance defect → state substitutes 'correct' management → controls assets in name of legality. Now applied to a university AND the main opposition party in the same week. Once a court provides the pretext, the state decides which documents and signatures it recognizes.
items.angle: Each application normalizes the template for the next target. The speed of escalation (3 days from standoff to police breach) and parallel application to a university in the same week suggests this is a campaign, not reactive. Turkey risk premium should structurally reprice.
items.tweetA: Same week Turkey's police stormed the opposition HQ: Erdogan also closed Istanbul's Bilgi University by presidential decree. Same judicial/administrative playbook, different institution. This is normalization of a state takeover toolkit being stress-tested across institution types.
items.tweetB: Turkey's 'trusteeship logic': court finds governance defect → state appoints correct management → seizes assets. Used on municipalities, universities, now the main opposition party. Each iteration makes the next one easier. How many institution types are left untested?

items.title: CHP Split Creates a Self-Sabotaging Feedback Loop Before the Next Election Window
items.idea: With two rival CHP leadership claims, provincial branches receive parallel orders, candidate selections are contested, and local party assets face competing legal claims. Every cycle drains resources into lawyers while freezing decision-making — exactly when an opposition must mobilize. Next hard trigger: days-to-weeks for signature recognition (controls payroll, directives, congress scheduling). If the state refuses to pause enforcement while appeals run, the legal process lane is effectively closed.
items.angle: The split opposition is a structural advantage to Erdogan even without further action — the legal/administrative fog alone drains the opposition's resources and coordination capacity.
items.tweetA: Turkey's opposition now has two rival leaderships giving parallel orders to the same provincial branches. Competing candidate filings. Litigation over local assets. Every court date costs money and freezes decisions. Erdogan doesn't need to do anything else — the opposition is burning its own fuel.
items.tweetB: When does a political party stop being a party? When it can't authorize payroll, convene a lawful congress, or file candidate papers. Turkey just created that situation for CHP by controlling the building and bank accounts. The 2023 congress wasn't just annulled — the party's ability to function was.

items.title: Trump Blinked on Iran — Markets Marked the Bottom the Moment He Did
items.idea: Earlier in the week of May 19-24, markets wobbled on speculation the US was planning military escalation against Iran alongside a sharp rise in bond volatility. Trump ultimately decided not to follow through — and that decision practically marked the week's bottom. From there: Small Caps +270bps, Spooz +88bps. The signal: geopolitical risk premium is being repriced in real-time with military decision flow, not subsequent news.
items.angle: We're in a regime where CENTCOM decisions matter more than FOMC decisions at the margin. Any Iran de-escalation signal is a buy catalyst; any escalation threat is a sell trigger. With Hormuz still reportedly blocked, the next catalyst is another Trump decision point on Iran.
items.tweetA: Trump reportedly had an Iran attack planned last week. He didn't follow through. Markets hit their bottom the moment that news spread and rallied hard from there. We are now in a regime where military decision-making IS the marginal driver of equity markets. The Fed matters less than CENTCOM.
items.tweetB: Small caps +270bps this week vs Spooz +88bps. Why the divergence? Bond volatility collapsed once the Iran escalation came off the table. IWM is the most rate-sensitive major index — it is now a real-time tracker of geopolitical tail risk premiums.

charts.desc: SPY, QQQ, IWM, DJIA weekly standard deviation channel charts — context for the week's range and ATH positioning

items.title: Growth the Only Negative Factor This Week — Quiet Rotation to Value Beneath ATH
items.idea: For the week ending May 24, Small-Cap Value, Low-Volatility, and Quality were the best-performing factors. Growth was the only factor to close the week negative. This happened despite major indices closing at or near all-time highs. IWM led (+270bps) while SPY lagged (+88bps). The breadth story inside the ATH: the most interest-rate-sensitive names won while growth/duration names lost.
items.angle: Bond vol drove this factor rotation, not fundamentals. If Thursday's PCE prints hot, bond vol re-emerges and this entire factor picture reverses — Growth rips back, small caps give it up. The factor trade is a levered bet on rate stability.
items.tweetA: Growth was the ONLY factor negative this week. Small-Cap Value, Low-Vol, Quality all outperformed — at all-time highs. This is not a euphoric risk-on rally. It's a quality/defensives bid embedded in an ATH tape. The market is more cautious than the headline number suggests.
items.tweetB: IWM +270bps. SPY +88bps. At the same time. The spread between them is the rate-volatility trade. Bond vol compressed → small caps rip. If Thursday's PCE is hot, bond vol explodes and this reverses instantly. The week's biggest trade was really a bet on Iran de-escalation via rates.

items.title: ATH Without Euphoria — Fear-Greed Slowly Drifting Back to Neutral at All-Time Highs
items.idea: Despite S&P at all-time highs, the Fear-Greed Index is slowly returning to 'Neutral' from 'Greed' territory. 51% of stocks are above the 20-day MA (neutral — was at 35% in short-term oversold weeks ago). 57% are above the 50D, also neutral. The most recent rally leg has been narrow — a concentrated set of names, not broad participation.
items.angle: Neutral sentiment + ATH price = significant dry powder still on sidelines. Historically consistent with a 'wall of worry' melt-up rather than a distribution top. The risk: the rally's narrowness means breadth confirmation is the missing ingredient.
items.tweetA: S&P at all-time highs. Fear-Greed Index slowly moving BACK toward Neutral from Greed. That is the opposite pattern from what you see at major tops. This has the structure of a wall-of-worry climb, not a blowoff. There is a lot of room for sentiment to expand before this peaks.
items.tweetB: Only 51% of stocks above their 20-day moving average while indices print ATHs. The rally is real — the participation is not broad. We have been here before: late 2023, early 2024. It doesn't mean it breaks, but breadth needs to widen for the move to be durable. PCE Thursday is the first test.

charts.desc: 🔥 CHART WORTH SAVING — Fear-Greed Index historical overlay vs S&P 500. Shows current 'Neutral' reading against prior ATH episodes. Tells the wall-of-worry story visually.

items.title: PCE Thursday — The Only Data Point That Matters in a Shortened Holiday Week
items.idea: The week of May 25 (Memorial Day) is shortened with thin volumes. The sole significant release is Thursday's PCE print — the Fed's preferred inflation gauge. Context: indices at ATHs, factor rotation toward Value/defensives, breadth neutral (51% above 20D), Iran de-escalation driving IWM. A hot PCE would re-ignite bond volatility, reverse the week's factor trade, and compress narrow breadth further.
items.angle: Single dominant data point + holiday-thin volume + indices at ATH = potential for outsized moves either direction. Small cap/rate-sensitive positions are most exposed to a hot surprise.
items.tweetA: Shortened week. Holiday volumes. One data point matters: PCE Thursday. The Fed's preferred inflation gauge. A hot print into a thin market with indices at ATH and factor positioning tilted toward defensives is a setup for a quick, sharp repricing in rate-sensitive names.
items.tweetB: Markets just rallied 270bps in IWM off an Iran de-escalation. Bond vol is compressed. If PCE Thursday is hot, the rate volatility that has been suppressed comes back fast and the small-cap trade gives it all back in one session. Low liquidity amplifies both directions.

items.title: Industrial Sovereignty #2 YTD, Global Marketplace #1 Worst — Tariff Trade Is Permanent
items.idea: Top YTD Plutus baskets: #1 Industrial & Auto Analog Recovery, #2 Rebuilding US Industrial Sovereignty, #3 Google Ecosystem, #4 Next-Gen Space Economy, #5 Agentic Economy. Bottom: #1 Global Marketplace, #2 Gaming & Media, #3 Make Housing Great Again. Domestic manufacturing reindustrialization and AI infrastructure outperforms globalization plays. The divergence between Industrial Sovereignty (#2) and Global Marketplace (#1 worst) captures the tariff thesis in a single cross-basket spread.
items.angle: The market is pricing the tariff regime as structural, not transitory. If a US-China deal materializes, this cross-basket spread is the most obvious pair trade to fade.
items.tweetA: Year-to-date best performing baskets: Industrial/Auto Recovery, US Industrial Sovereignty, Google Ecosystem, Space Economy, Agentic Economy. Worst: Global Marketplace, Gaming, Housing. The tariff trade is not being faded — the market is pricing it as the new permanent regime.
items.tweetB: The best single expression of the tariff trade: long Industrial Sovereignty, short Global Marketplace. That spread is the #2 best vs the #1 worst performing basket YTD. If you think trade normalizes, that's your fade. If you think it doesn't, stay long reindustrialization.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz to Stay Blocked Until a Deal Is Reached
items.idea: Seven C Newsletter reports the Strait of Hormuz will remain blocked until a diplomatic agreement is reached. The Strait handles approximately 17-20mb/d of oil and LNG — roughly 20% of global traded energy. An extended blockade of this duration is historically unprecedented in the post-1979 era. This coincides with Iran reportedly using Bitcoin for shipping payments (see item 10), signaling Iran is preparing for a prolonged standoff, not a quick resolution.
items.angle: Duration matters more than the blockade fact. Crack spreads and tanker rates are the real-time signals — if refiners start pricing in multi-week disruption, crude flat price follows. The Iran de-escalation that marked last week's equity bottom may prove temporary if Hormuz stays shut.
items.tweetA: Hormuz stays blocked until there is a deal. Not a day. Not a week. Until a deal. That is 20% of global oil trade held hostage to diplomatic resolution. Crack spreads are the canary. If refiners start pricing duration, the crude price responds whether or not futures traders believe the headline.
items.tweetB: Markets rallied last week when Trump backed off the Iran attack. But Hormuz is still shut. The equity market is pricing de-escalation; the energy market is pricing disruption. One of them is wrong. I know which one I trust when physical supply is the constraint.

items.title: Iran Starts Bitcoin-Backed Shipping — Sanctions Just Created a Live Crypto Use Case
items.idea: Seven C Newsletter reports Iran has started using Bitcoin to back and settle international shipping payments — a direct sanctions workaround. This is a live demonstration of the thesis that when the dollar is weaponized through sanctions, non-sovereign monetary assets gain utility. If Iranian shipping companies are settling in BTC, a parallel financial system for sanctioned trade is being built one trade at a time. Same week: Hormuz shut while crypto-based payment rails are being constructed.
items.angle: Iran using BTC for shipping = Bitcoin is a geopolitical settlement layer, not just a speculative asset. This is how parallel financial systems form — under sanctions pressure, quietly, creating facts on the ground. Russia, North Korea, Venezuela are watching. This is adoption.
items.tweetA: Iran just started using Bitcoin to settle shipping payments. Not a pilot. Not a futures contract. Live shipping trades. This is how Bitcoin becomes a geopolitical reserve asset — not through ETF adoption, but through sanctions pressure forcing non-sovereign settlement.
items.tweetB: Every sanctions regime strengthens the case for non-sovereign money. Iran, Russia, North Korea all finding BTC utility. The dollar is the world's most powerful financial weapon AND its biggest strategic vulnerability — every use of that weapon accelerates the search for alternatives.

items.title: SpaceX Files for IPO and Discloses Bitcoin Holdings on Its Balance Sheet
items.idea: Seven C Newsletter reports SpaceX has filed for IPO and disclosed Bitcoin holdings in its filing. If confirmed, SpaceX would become the first major space/defense company to list publicly with crypto balance sheet exposure. SpaceX's recent secondary market valuation exceeds $200B — one of the largest IPOs in history. The BTC disclosure adds a new dimension: SpaceX stock becomes a crypto proxy for institutional investors who cannot hold Bitcoin directly.
items.angle: SpaceX IPO + BTC disclosure is a landmark for institutional crypto. Musk is replicating the MicroStrategy playbook at a company running global military communications, satellite internet, and rocket infrastructure. SpaceX stock would immediately be the largest liquid crypto proxy for TradFi.
items.tweetA: SpaceX files for IPO. Discloses Bitcoin on the balance sheet. At a $200B+ valuation this is one of the largest IPOs ever — and it comes with crypto exposure baked in. SpaceX stock will immediately become the largest liquid crypto proxy for institutions that cannot hold BTC directly.
items.tweetB: SpaceX + BTC disclosure is Elon Musk doing to aerospace what MicroStrategy did to enterprise software. The difference: SpaceX runs Starlink, GPS-grade military comms, and Mars vehicle development. With BTC on the balance sheet. This changes the risk asset correlation map.

items.title: Bitcoin Retested 200-Week MA at $67.2k in Feb 2026 — Held, But No All-Clear Signal Yet
items.idea: Cubic Analytics went bearish in November 2025 when BTC broke below $97k, invalidating the 2-day 200 MA cloud. Their forecast: every BTC bear market retests the 200-week MA cloud. In January-February 2026, BTC bottomed around $67.2k — exactly that level. They started buying there. Critical unresolved signal: BTC has NOT yet reclaimed the 2-day 200 MA cloud — the 'all clear' for a new bull run has NOT been given. A new study that 'hasn't flashed yet' is forthcoming.
items.angle: $67.2k held = 200-week MA structural support confirmed. 2-day 200 MA cloud not reclaimed = still a recovery trade, not a confirmed new bull. Watch the 2-day 200 MA cloud reclaim as the binary on/off switch for the next leg. If it breaks $67.2k, the floor is gone.
items.tweetA: Bitcoin retested its 4-year (200-week) moving average at $67.2k in February 2026. Every prior BTC bear market had this retest. Cubic Analytics called it in November, bought at $67.2k. It held. Now the only question is whether BTC reclaims the 2-day 200 MA cloud — that is the true all-clear signal.
items.tweetB: The Bitcoin playbook: sell the 2-day 200 MA cloud break (Nov 25 at $97k), buy the 200-week MA retest ($67.2k Feb 26). Simple rules, brutal discipline. But the 2-day 200 MA cloud still has not been reclaimed. We are technically still in a bear market recovery, not a new bull.

charts.desc: Charts inside — worth opening: 2-day 200 MA cloud vs price, 200-week MA retest annotation

items.title: Eliant Portfolio +192% Since Jun 2023 vs QQQ +104%, 81.9% Win Rate — Active Alpha Still Exists
items.idea: Eliant reports their newsletter/portfolio has returned +192.39% since June 2023 vs QQQ +103.89%, SPX +78.93%, Dow +57.23%, IWM +63.03%. Stats: 81.9% win rate, 29.34% average gain on winners, 15.73% average loss on losers — a 1.87:1 win/loss ratio. The outperformance is nearly 2:1 vs the best-performing major index (QQQ). Current best baskets: Industrials, Space Economy, Agentic Economy, US Industrial Sovereignty.
items.angle: This regime — where specific thematic and factor positioning can massively outperform passive — is worth understanding. The factor/thematic selection (reindustrialization, AI infrastructure, space) captured structural shifts that index exposure could not. Signal about what kind of market 2023-26 has been.
items.tweetA: A Substack newsletter has returned +192% since June 2023 vs QQQ +104%, S&P +79%, IWM +63%. 81.9% win rate. 29% avg gain vs 16% avg loss. The narrative that active management cannot beat the market has exceptions — and the exceptions cluster in specific thematic/factor regimes.
items.tweetB: 1.87:1 win/loss ratio. 82% win rate. The environment: reindustrialization, AI buildout, geopolitical factor rotation from 2023-2026. The question is whether the edge survives when the regime changes. PCE Thursday might be the first test of whether this market still rewards the same themes.
